steps.title: Simplify each equation
steps.content: Equation 1 is already $x+6y=-14$. Dividing equation 2 by 5 gives $x+6y=0$. Dividing equation 3 by 2 gives $x+6y=19.5$.

steps.title: Compare the lines
steps.content: All three equations have the same left side, so they have the same slope, but their constants are different. They are three distinct parallel lines, so there is no point that satisfies all three equations.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Zero matches three distinct parallel lines with no common point.
  • Exactly one would require the system to meet at a single shared point, which parallel distinct lines cannot do.
  • Exactly two is not possible for a system of linear equations.
  • Infinitely many would require all three equations to represent the same line, but the constants $-14$, $0$, and $19.5$ differ.
That matches A.
